LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT KIMPTON HOTELS AND RESTAURANTS GROUP LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF REQUESTED LEAVE TO AMEND IN ORDER TO ASSERT AN ADDITIONAL REMEDY. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALINGS ONLY PROTECTS BENEFITS INCORPORATED IN THE SPECIFIC TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE THE CONTRACT PROVISIONS, OR FACTS SUPPORTING IMPLIED-IN-FACT CONTRACT PROVISIONS, THAT ENTITLED PLAINTIFF TO A BENEFIT THAT DEFENDANT VIOLATED. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE CONDITIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT WERE MATERIALLY ALTERED WHEN SHE WAS ASKED TO LEAVE HER POSITION AND NOT COME TO WORK. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976
LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT GEORGE SANTORA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, SET FOR SEP-5-2012, IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT SANTORA, GEORGE
OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF HORLAK, RACHAEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GEORGE SANTORA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE DISCHARGE. THAT CAUSE OF ACTION IS ACTUALLY A CLAIM FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ALLEGE THAT CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT SANTORA BECAUSE THE PARTIES DO NOT SHARE AN EMPLOYER-EMPLOYEE RELATIONSHIP. THE DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ("NIED") IS OVERRULED. THE COURT RECOGNIZES THAT NIED IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION. UNDER CCP 452, THE COURT LIBERALLY READS THE CLAIM AS A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS THE EXISTENCE OF A DUTY PURSUANT TO THE COMMON LAW AND FEHA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2012 Continued To Dec-19-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David A. Lucero, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, Tel. 415.438.6641, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GEORGE SANTORA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action for constructive discharge. That cause of action is actually a claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Plaintiff cannot allege that claim against defendant Santora because the parties do not share an employer-employee relationship. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress ("NIED") is overruled. The Court recognizes that NIED is not a distinct cause of action. Under CCP 452, the Court liberally reads the claim as a cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the existence of a duty pursuant to the common law and FEHA. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KIMPTON HOTELS AND RESTAURANTS GROUP LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained in part. The demurrer to the third and fifth causes of action are sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff requested leave to amend in order to assert an additional remedy. As to the fifth cause of action, the covenant of good faith and fair dealings only protects benefits incorporated in the specific terms of the contract. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if she can do so in good faith, to allege the contract provisions, or facts supporting implied-in-fact contract provisions, that entitled Plaintiff to a benefit that Defendant violated. The demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action, the conditions of Plaintiff's employment were materially altered when she was asked to leave her position and not come to work. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
